goal: Replace placeholder copy with final Greek-primary content; wire production SEO (per-route meta, OG image, sitemap.xml, robots.txt) and privacy-respecting analytics.
why: The demo is live and public but search engines see one static title across all 12 routes, link previews have no image, crawlers have no sitemap, and there is zero usage signal. This phase makes the public surface discoverable and measurable without redesigning anything.
success_criteria: Each public route emits a unique, accurate Greek-primary <title> + meta description.
/sitemap.xml and /robots.txt are served and valid; /opengraph-image returns a 1200x630 PNG that renders in a link-preview check.
Analytics fires a pageview on navigation when NEXT_PUBLIC_ANALYTICS_DOMAIN is set, and renders nothing when unset.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; src/lib/i18n.ts unchanged (EL/EN stays 218/218); no env file or analytics key committed.

tasks.title: sitemap.ts + robots.ts (crawl surface)
acceptance_criteria: /sitemap.xml returns valid XML with one url loc per indexable route, each URL built from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL (no hardcoded domain).
/robots.txt returns User-Agent * with Allow /, Disallow covering private app surfaces, and a Sitemap line pointing at the NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL sitemap.xml.
tasks.action: Create src/app/sitemap.ts (default export sitemap returning MetadataRoute.Sitemap) listing public routes /, /mechanic, /register, /welcome, /login, /signup, /book with url built from process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL; exclude session-scoped surfaces. Create src/app/robots.ts (default export robots returning MetadataRoute.Robots) with userAgent star, allow /, disallow private surfaces, sitemap pointing at base sitemap.xml using the same env base.

tasks.title: opengraph-image.tsx (link-preview image)
acceptance_criteria: A request to /opengraph-image returns a 1200x630 PNG.
The image matches the shipped brand: barely-tinted neutral ground, the Costas C mark in indigo accent, Greek-primary tagline; no colors outside the DESIGN.md palette.
tasks.action: Create src/app/opengraph-image.tsx: import ImageResponse from next/og; export alt (Greek), size width 1200 height 630, contentType image/png; default-export Image returning new ImageResponse(JSX, size). Use inline-style literal palette hex (#fbfbfc ground, #111114 foreground, #4f46e5 accent, #71757e muted) since Satori cannot resolve CSS vars; every flex container gets explicit display flex. Do NOT load a Geist ttf (none bundled) — use default font.

tasks.title: Per-route Greek-primary metadata (unique title + description on every public route)
acceptance_criteria: Each of the 12 public routes emits a unique title and a non-empty meta description, written Greek-primary.
No two routes share the same title.
The 4 client routes (login, signup, forgot-password, welcome) get title/description from a new sibling layout.tsx, not from the client page.tsx.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0; no new i18n keys (EL/EN stays 218/218).
tasks.action: Add/extend export const metadata Metadata = { title, description } on each server page with distinct Greek-primary strings; add robots index false on settings/admin/reminders/auth-reset-password. Create a server-component sibling layout.tsx for each of login/signup/forgot-password/welcome (which are use client and cannot export metadata) exporting metadata and returning children. Do NOT touch src/lib/i18n.ts — these are static SEO strings, not UI copy.

tasks.title: Root metadata defaults (metadataBase + OG) + privacy-respecting analytics
acceptance_criteria: Root metadata sets metadataBase from NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL, a title default plus template, and an openGraph object (type website, locale el_GR, siteName Costas).
The OG image from Task 2 appears as og:image once metadataBase is set.
Analytics renders nothing (returns null, no script) when NEXT_PUBLIC_ANALYTICS_DOMAIN is unset; when set, injects one next/script loader and fires a pageview on navigation.
No analytics key/domain hardcoded; nothing secret committed.
tasks.action: Create src/components/Analytics.tsx (server component): read NEXT_PUBLIC_ANALYTICS_DOMAIN; return null when falsy; else render Plausible via next/script (defer, data-domain, plausible.io script, afterInteractive). Extend root metadata in layout.tsx: metadataBase from NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL, title default plus template, openGraph (type, locale el_GR, siteName, title, description). Render Analytics last in body. Add the env var to .env.local only.
